structuralIssues.title: EAD expiry (2031) vs SReq timeline (2029) creates regulatory gap risk
structuralIssues.description: EADs expire 9 Jan 2031 but SReq not planned until 2029. Only ~2 years between SReq adoption and EAD expiry — typically the full SReq→development→citation cycle takes 3-4 years. This means new hENs will NOT be available before EADs expire. While ETAs remain valid until 2036 (Art. 95(4) backstop), no new ETAs can be issued after 2031. This creates a gap where new products cannot enter the market via the EAD route and new hENs are not yet available.
structuralIssues.action_required: Flag for policy monitoring. Consider whether Pipeline A NT-9 estimated date (~2030-2032) is realistic given the SReq is planned for 2029.

structuralIssues.title: Five content sections empty
structuralIssues.description: content.standards_landscape, content.standards_development, content.stakeholder_notes, content.key_risks, and content.sources_summary are all empty. FPP has significant context worth documenting: EAD-only status (0 hENs), 800+ active ETAs, PFAS exposure, CEN/TC 127 test method context, timeline gap risk.
structuralIssues.action_required: Populate with proposed content from deep-dive report §8.

structuralIssues.title: FPP is bellwether for EAD-only family transition
structuralIssues.description: FPP's all-EAD structure (0 hENs, 6 EADs) makes it a test case for the EAD sunset pathway under CPR 2024/3110. Lessons from FPP's EAD→hEN transition planning will directly inform other EAD-heavy families: FIX (Batch 6), KAS (Batch 6), SEA (Batch 6).
structuralIssues.action_required: Cross-reference when FIX, KAS, SEA analyzed in Batch 6.

structuralIssues.title: PFAS broader restriction SEAC consultation spring 2026
structuralIssues.description: Current EU PFAS restriction (Regulation (EU) 2025/1988) targets firefighting foams only, effective October 2026. Broader PFAS restriction under development: ECHA SEAC draft opinion consultation spring 2026, final opinions expected end of 2026. If broader restriction is adopted, it could affect PFAS-containing fire retardant products (EAD 350865-00-1106) and intumescent coatings (EAD 350005-00-1104) in FPP scope. Timeline and scope uncertain but regulatory direction toward broader restriction is clear.
structuralIssues.action_required: Monitor ECHA SEAC consultation spring 2026 for construction product impacts. Flag for fire retardant and intumescent product manufacturers.

structuralIssues.title: Split EPD readiness: intumescent coatings have EPDs, penetration seals likely don't
structuralIssues.description: Intumescent fire protection coatings have verified EPDs: Nullifire SC802/SC803/SC804 (EPD International S-P-13759), Sherwin-Williams Firetex M71V2 (NSF EPD10559), Rudolf Hensel 410KS/420KS/421KS (EN 15804+A2). However, penetration seals (EAD 350454), linear joint/gap seals (EAD 350141/350142), and fire retardant products (EAD 350865) appear to lack EPDs. Split EPD readiness within the FPP family means DPP environmental data will be easier for some product types than others.
structuralIssues.source: EPD International Nullifire (https://www.environdec.com/library/epd13759); NSF Firetex (https://info.nsf.org/Certified/Sustain/ProdCert/EPD10559.pdf); Rudolf Hensel (https://www.rudolf-hensel.de/wp-content/uploads/download/EPD_410KS_420KS_421KS_GB.pdf)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document split EPD readiness in content. Note intumescent coatings as best-positioned sub-category.

structuralIssues.title: Dual chemical reformulation pressure: PFAS + brominated FR restrictions
structuralIssues.description: FPP faces chemical reformulation risk on two fronts: (1) PFAS restriction (Regulation (EU) 2025/1988) — currently targets foams only but broader SEAC consultation spring 2026 may extend to construction; (2) ECHA proposes 'wide and generic restriction' on aromatic brominated flame retardants under REACH — affects fire retardant products (EAD 350865). Industry shifting to halogen-free alternatives and water-based formulations (35.3% market share 2024). Reformulated products may require new ETAs.
structuralIssues.source: Pinfa FR regulation plans (https://www.pinfa.eu/news/plans-for-fr-assessment-and-regulation/); Stellarix halogen-free (https://stellarix.com/insights/blogs/halogen-free-flame-retardants-implementation-complexities/)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document dual chemical reformulation risk in content.key_risks. Monitor ECHA SEAC and brominated FR restriction dossier.

structuralIssues.title: Hilti BIM/digital infrastructure provides DPP data architecture groundwork
structuralIssues.description: Hilti firestop division has developed digital infrastructure paralleling DPP needs: 'Button for Firestop' (3D BIM design automation), 'Firestop Documentation Manager' (jobsite documentation/reports), CFS-CT products on NBS Source (BIM platform), CAD/BIM Typicals. This proprietary digital data infrastructure demonstrates fire stopping products CAN be structured digitally. Digital-ready manufacturers (Hilti, Promat) have significant advantage in DPP transition vs manufacturers without digital infrastructure.
structuralIssues.source: Hilti Firestop Design Center (https://www.hilti.com/content/hilti/W1/US/en/business/business/engineering/fire-protection.html); NBS Source CFS-CT (https://source.thenbs.com/product/cfs-ct-b-firestop-board/9DohpdSwQGrzP9BuPf9JNy/u2q9uKBReerPT4uroJAwNR)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document BIM/digital readiness in content. Note as positive DPP indicator for leading manufacturers.
